name: Ma Chao
description: Ma Chao, also known as Ma Mengqi, was a prominent general who lived during the late Eastern Han dynasty and early Three Kingdoms period of China. He was the eldest son of Ma Teng, a warlord who controlled Liang Province (涼州; covering parts of present-day Gansu and Shaanxi). Ma Chao inherited his father's martial prowess and was renowned for his exceptional skill in battle, earning him the nickname "Ma Chao the Splendid" for his valiant and fearsome fighting style. After Cao Cao assassinated Ma Teng, Ma Chao, fueled by vengeance, formed a coalition with Han Sui and other warlords to challenge Cao Cao's dominance. He led a formidable army and inflicted significant defeats on Cao Cao's forces, even nearly killing Cao Cao himself at the Battle of Tong Pass. Despite his initial successes, Ma Chao was eventually defeated and forced to flee, seeking refuge under various warlords, including Zhang Lu, before finally joining Liu Bei. He became one of Liu Bei's Five Tiger Generals, recognized for his bravery and contributions to the establishment of the Shu Han state. Ma Chao's legend persists as a symbol of courage, loyalty (despite his initial actions), and martial excellence in Chinese culture, particularly in the Romance of the Three Kingdoms, where he is heavily romanticized and portrayed as a near-invincible warrior.
subtitle: Splendid General of the Five Tigers
achievements: Nearly defeated Cao Cao at Tong Pass
Became one of Liu Bei's Five Tiger Generals
Led a formidable coalition against Cao Cao
Known for his exceptional martial prowess

keyMyths.title: Battle of Tong Pass
keyMyths.description: Ma Chao's most famous battle where he nearly killed Cao Cao, showcasing his incredible fighting abilities and vengeful spirit. This battle solidified his reputation as a formidable warrior.

keyMyths.title: Joining Liu Bei
keyMyths.description: After facing defeat and betrayal, Ma Chao eventually joined Liu Bei's forces, recognizing his potential and aligning with his ideals. He became a valuable asset to Liu Bei's cause.

keyMyths.title: Five Tiger Generals
keyMyths.description: Ma Chao was recognized as one of the Five Tiger Generals of Shu, a testament to his military prowess and his contribution to Liu Bei's campaigns. This honor cemented his legacy as a legendary hero.

sources.author: Luo Guanzhong
sources.description: A historical novel that heavily romanticizes Ma Chao's life and exploits.
sources.text: A historical novel that heavily romanticizes Ma Chao's life and exploits.

sources.author: Chen Shou
